text.title: DDD Repositories Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to introduce a Repository for a new aggregate.

step.name: define-interface
step.action: Create `<Aggregate>Repository` abstract class/interface in `domain/<context>/repository.py`. Methods: `find_by_id`, `save`, `delete`, optional `find_by_external_key`. No ORM imports.
step.decision-gate: If the interface imports any ORM type, FAIL.

step.name: write-mapper
step.action: Define `<Aggregate>Mapper` in infrastructure with `to_aggregate(model) -> Aggregate` and `to_model(aggregate, existing=None) -> OrmModel`. Handle nested entities + value objects.

step.name: implement-adapter
step.action: Implement `SqlAlchemy<Aggregate>Repository` (or framework equivalent). Eager-load relations; return aggregates; on save, merge mapper output into the session and `flush()` (commit handled by app service).

step.name: in-memory-double
step.action: Write `InMemory<Aggregate>Repository` implementing the same port. Used by domain unit tests + integration tests that don't need DB fidelity.

step.name: wire-and-test
step.action: Bind interface → adapter in DI container. Write a contract test asserting both implementations satisfy the same invariants (`save then find_by_id returns equal aggregate`).
step.decision-gate: If domain tests need the DB adapter, the interface is being misused — refactor.
